title: Express Routing Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of HTTP methods (GET/POST), dynamic route parameters (:id), and query parameters for feature-rich web applications.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the primarily role of 'Routing' in an Express.js application?
options: Defining which code should run when a user visits a specific URL or performs a specific action (like a POST request).
To hide the JavaScript code from the console.

questions.question: Which and/or what was the primarily method used to create a dynamic route variable like a user ID?
options: Using a colon (:) in the route definition (e.g., /users/:id).

questions.question: Which property in the 'req' object would you use in Express to access a dynamic route parameter like ':id'?
Which and/or what property in the 'req' object would you use to access 'Query Parameters' (like /search?q=node)?
Does Express allowing the use of multiple handlers for a single route using the 'next()' function?
options: Yes, as it is a core feature of the Express middleware system.
No, only one handler per route is allowed.
